config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena hak individu adalah batas agar kekuasaan tidak semena-mena. Dari pengalaman saya melihat kehidupan di Indonesia, ketika kepentingan umum dijadikan alasan tanpa kontrol, yang sering dirugikan justru orang kecil dan kelompok lemah. Yang lebih tepat adalah mencari keseimbangan, bukan menempatkan masyarakat di atas individu secara mutlak.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The rights of the individual should not be subordinated to society as a default, because that is exactly how abuse of power starts. From an American perspective, I place a lot of weight on constitutional rights, free speech, due process, and limits on government, since history shows that “for the common good” can become a justification for trampling minorities and unpopular people. That said, individual rights are not absolute in every case; public safety, disease control, and similar issues can justify limits, but those limits need to be narrow, evidence-based, and accountable. So I do not accept the statement as written: society matters, but it should not automatically take priority over the individual.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju dengan pernyataan itu, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan hak orang kecil, bukan benar-benar melindungi publik. Pengalaman kita menunjukkan bahwa tanpa batas yang jelas, hak individu mudah dikorbankan atas nama ketertiban, pembangunan, atau moralitas mayoritas. Hak individu memang tidak absolut, tetapi justru harus jadi pagar agar negara dan kelompok mayoritas tidak semena-mena. Jadi, yang tepat bukan mendahulukan masyarakat secara otomatis, melainkan menimbang keduanya dengan adil dan transparan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The statement goes too far for me, because in the United States I do not trust “society first” as a default rule. I have seen how easily that language can be used to excuse censorship, discrimination, and government overreach, especially against minorities or unpopular speakers. Public safety and the common good do matter, but they have to be justified case by case, with clear limits and real accountability. So I would put individual rights first unless there is a narrow, compelling reason to restrict them.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu karena di Indonesia alasan “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan terhadap orang kecil atau minoritas. Bagi saya, hak individu harus menjadi batas utama agar negara dan mayoritas tidak semena-mena, walaupun dalam situasi tertentu seperti bencana atau kesehatan publik pembatasan memang bisa dibenarkan. Masalahnya bukan pada adanya kepentingan bersama, melainkan pada kecenderungan kita menganggapnya otomatis lebih suci daripada hak orang per orang. Jadi, yang benar adalah menyeimbangkan keduanya dengan alasan yang jelas, bukan menempatkan masyarakat di atas individu secara mutlak.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the statement, because from an American perspective it is too easy for “society first” to become a blank check for government overreach and majoritarian abuse. In the United States, I value individual rights as the starting point precisely because history shows that minorities, dissenters, and unpopular people are the first to get sacrificed when the common good is treated as automatically superior. I do think society can sometimes justify limits, but only in narrow, accountable cases like public health or immediate safety, not as a general rule. So for me, the burden should be on society to prove why a restriction is necessary, not on the individual to surrender rights by default.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
